items.headline: Tech Sell-Off Drags Nasdaq and S&P 500 Lower Amid Strong Jobs Report, Dow Shows Resilience
items.summary: U.S. equities experienced a mixed session on Friday, June 5, 2026, as a stronger-than-expected May jobs report fueled concerns about potential Federal Reserve rate hikes, triggering a significant sell-off in technology stocks. The Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 closed lower, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average, after an earlier surge to a record high, retreated to close down, but showed some underlying resilience from banking and retail sectors. Overall market sentiment is under pressure due to fatigue in the AI-led equity rally and shifting expectations for monetary policy.

items.headline: Solidion Technology Leads Monthly Gains
items.summary: Solidion Technology, Inc. ($STI) has been the best performing stock in June, showing strong momentum.

items.headline: Laser Photonics Continues Upward Trend
items.summary: Laser Photonics Corporation ($LASE) is among the top performers for June 2026.

items.headline: Indaptus Therapeutics Rallies
items.summary: Indaptus Therapeutics, Inc. ($INDP) recorded a substantial monthly gain.

items.headline: Strong May Jobs Report Beats Expectations, Dims Rate Cut Hopes
items.summary: The U.S. Labor Department reported that total nonfarm payroll employment increased by 172,000 in May, significantly higher than economists' forecasts. The unemployment rate remained steady at 4.3%. This robust labor market data has led to reduced expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts this year, with markets now largely pricing in a rate hike before year-end.

items.headline: Technology Sector Faces Sell-Off as AI Rally Shows Fatigue
items.summary: The technology sector experienced a significant downturn, with major players like Broadcom ($AVGO) falling 12.6% after disappointing earnings and unchanged full-year AI chip targets. Nvidia ($NVDA) and Micron Technology ($MU) also saw notable declines. This broad sell-off is indicative of profit-taking and a reassessment of the artificial intelligence trade, leading investors to question if growth rates have peaked.

items.headline: Investors Rotate Towards Cyclical, Healthcare, and Financial Sectors
items.summary: Amid the tech sector's struggles, there was a notable rotation of investor capital into more stable and cyclical sectors. Healthcare stocks, represented by the Health Care Select Sector SPDR ($XLV), gained 3.1%, while Financials Select Sector SPDR ($XLF) rose 2.6%. The Dow's performance was bolstered by strong showings in banking and retail, particularly from UnitedHealth ($UNH) and JPMorgan ($JPM).

items.headline: Inflation Concerns Persist, Fed Focuses on Private Credit Exposure
items.summary: Inflation data remains elevated, contributing to the Federal Reserve's 'on hold' posture for interest rates. Additionally, the Fed has launched a new data-collection effort to gain greater transparency into banks' lending to the private credit sector, reflecting growing regulatory focus on potential systemic risks. Geopolitical tensions, particularly the ongoing Iran conflict, continue to impact oil prices and contribute to broader economic uncertainty.

items.headline: Key Earnings: GameStop Reports Record Profits, Lululemon Trims Guidance
items.summary: In corporate earnings news, GameStop reported its highest quarterly net income in company history, with net sales up 14%. In contrast, Lululemon ($LULU) saw its shares fall after trimming its full-year revenue and profit forecasts, highlighting challenges in the athletic apparel retail sector.
